key_insight: Single-cell profiling revealed that renal cell carcinoma subtypes and metastatic progression are driven by distinct, dynamic gene expression programs rather than a single uniform tumor state.
clinical_relevance: These findings may improve RCC subtype classification, biomarker discovery, and risk stratification by identifying transcriptional programs linked to misclassification, poor outcome, and metastasis.
method: Analyzed 85,000+ single-cell gene expression profiles from primary tumors, metastases, and patient-derived RCC models.
Used generative modeling to separate true tumor programs from clonal and copy-number-driven expression shifts.
Mapped 59 gene expression programs and linked them to subtype identity, regulators, and clinical outcomes.
evidence_signal: The study integrates a large cross-subtype single-cell compendium spanning primary and metastatic RCC and reports subtype-specific biomarkers plus outcome-associated gene programs.
watch_item: Because the work is transcriptomic and classification-focused, findings will need external validation and prospective testing before they can guide routine clinical decision-making.
paper_title: Single-cell gene programs define subtype identity and metastatic trajectories in renal cell carcinoma
